sections.paragraph:
ἀκόλουθος, ον (Bacchylides, Soph.+) pert. to coming after someth. else in sequence, following (so Demosth. et al.; 2 Macc 4:17) καὶ τῶν λοιπῶν τῶν ἀκολούθων πάντων and of all the other things that followed Hs 5, 5, 1 (cod. A). W. gen. (Ps.-Demosth. 59, 8 τὰ ἀ. αὐτῶν; Just., D. 101, 1 τὰ ἀ. τοῦ ψαλμοῦ) τὰ ἀ. τούτων what follows them Hm 8:4, 10; cp. Hs 9, 15, 3. W. dat. (Lucian, Herm. 74; Ps.-Lucian: Anth. Pal. 11, 401, 4) τὰ τούτοις ἀ. (1 Esdr 8:14; Jos., Ant. 14, 1) and so forth MPol 8:2; 9:2.—Abs. ἀ. ἐστιν they follow from them (OGI 669, 32; PTebt 296, 14) IEph 14:1; it is suitable (Demosth. 46, 17; Ael. Aristid. 13 p. 172 D. al.; PTebt 304, 18 τὸ ἀ. ‘what is suitable’) ὅπου καὶ ἀ. ἦν where it was suitable = at a suitable place MPol 18:1.—DELG.

Bacchylides Bacchylides , V b.c.—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

someth. someth. = something

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Hs Hs = Similitudes

gen. gen. = genitive

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

dat. dat. = dative

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

Ael. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 37
